solutions.reasoning_steps:
Step 1: Analyzing Trigger Test and Error Message
The trigger test is designed to test the case where an interface extends non-existent interfaces. The error message is a NullPointerException that occurs when trying to get the property names of the implicit prototype of the interface type. This suggests that the implicit prototype is null, which is causing the exception.

Step 2: Analyzing Root Cause
Root Cause: The root cause of the bug is that the implicit prototype of the interface type is null when the interface extends a non-existent interface. This is because the getImplicitPrototype() method returns null when the interface type does not have an implicit prototype. When the code tries to get the property names of the implicit prototype using getOwnPropertyNames(), it throws a NullPointerException because it is trying to call a method on a null object.

Step 3: Exploring Potential Fixes
To fix this bug, we need to add a null check before trying to get the property names of the implicit prototype. We can do this by checking if the implicit prototype is null before trying to call getOwnPropertyNames() on it.
